food_name: Tteokbokki (Spicy Rice Cakes in Gochujang Sauce)
aliases: Spicy rice cakes in gochujang sauce

english: Spicy rice cakes in gochujang sauce

food_subgroup: Cylinder-shaped garae tteok (rice cake) in gochujang (fermented chilli paste) sauce with fishcake slices and boiled egg; 1 serving ~300g; GI ~85 (HIGH)

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn URI exists for Tteokbokki as a composite Korean street food dish. Closest parent would be a rice cake food product node; not assigned to avoid fabrication.
ontology.foodex2_description: Korean rice cake snack (closest parent match; no exact FoodEx2 code for tteokbokki composite dish)
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A037M used as closest parent for Korean rice cake snack. No exact composite tteokbokki code exists in FoodEx2 as of 2024.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code for tteokbokki; not a clinical substance requiring SNOMED mapping.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of tteokbokki is approximately 300g, equivalent to one plate as served at Korean street food stalls or restaurants, containing roughly 8–10 rice cake cylinders, 2–3 fishcake slices, and one boiled egg in gochujang sauce.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Rice cake (plain, brown rice) SR Legacy #20124 adapted for tteokbokki composite dish; supplemented with USDA FoodData Central fishcake and egg entries for composite estimation
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single USDA FoodData Central entry exists for tteokbokki as a composite dish. Values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy rice cake (#20124), USDA fishcake/fish paste entries, and USDA hard-boiled egg entry, weighted by approximate ingredient proportions (~55% rice cake, ~25% fishcake, ~10% egg, ~10% sauce). Sodium elevated to reflect gochujang sauce contribution (~400mg/100g composite). Values are consistent with the input record which cites USDA SR Legacy + Korean Nutrition Database (KNDB) cross-reference. Confidence: medium (adapted composite).

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of rice-based foods — analogous estimate from Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care 31(12):2281-2283; supplemented by Korean nutrition literature on garae tteok starch gelatinisation
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for tteokbokki composite dish exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI ~85 is estimated from the closest analogous food: glutinous/waxy rice products and rice cakes, which appear in the International Tables of GI Values (Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care) with GI values of 82–92 for rice cakes and glutinous rice preparations. The tteok-making process (high-pressure kneading/pounding of rice flour) destroys starch crystalline structure, producing a rapidly digestible starch with GI comparable to or exceeding plain rice cakes (GI ~82). GI ~85 is a defensible conservative estimate. GL per serving = (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (85 × 78g) / 100 = 66.3. Confidence: 0.9 (strong analogous basis).

hypertension.key_factor: Very high sodium content: ~1,200mg per 300g serving (400mg/100g), from gochujang sauce, processed fishcake, and soy sauce — all fully consumed with the dish
hypertension.guidance: Hypertensive patients should treat tteokbokki as an occasional food (maximum once every 2–3 weeks) and, where possible, request reduced fishcake portions or less sauce, as the sodium is fully embedded in the sauce and fishcake and cannot be separated at the point of consumption; a single serving provides approximately 52–60% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2,000mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Extremely high glycaemic load: GI ~85 (HIGH), GL ~66.3 per 300g serving — equivalent to 2.4× the glycaemic impact of a standard bowl of white rice; driven by rapidly digestible gelatinised rice starch and ~22g added sugar from gochujang sauce per serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid tteokbokki or limit intake to a very small portion (under 100g) paired with high-fibre vegetables and protein to blunt the postprandial glucose spike; even a half-serving (~150g) carries a glycaemic load equivalent to a full bowl of white rice, making this one of the highest-GL foods in the Korean cuisine category.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High sodium (~1,200mg/serving) and moderate saturated fat (~1.5g/serving) from fishcake and egg; low total fat (6g/serving) but processed fishcake contributes sodium and additives associated with cardiovascular risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: People with cardiovascular disease should limit tteokbokki to occasional consumption due to its high sodium load, which contributes to elevated blood pressure and cardiovascular risk; the dish is low in saturated fat relative to its caloric density, but the sodium content is the primary cardiovascular concern and cannot be reduced at the point of consumption.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Tteokbokki (떡볶이) is South Korea's most iconic street food — cylindrical garae tteok rice cakes simmered in a sweet-spicy gochujang (fermented red chilli paste) sauce, typically served with eomuk (fishcake slices) and a boiled egg. The rice cakes are made from non-glutinous rice flour that is pounded and kneaded under high pressure, producing a dense, chewy, slightly elastic texture. The gochujang sauce is sweet, spicy, and deeply savoury, combining fermented chilli paste with corn syrup, sugar, soy sauce, and water. Per 300g serving: approximately 450 kcal, 12g protein, 6g fat, 78g carbohydrate (including ~22g added sugar from the sauce), GI ~85 (HIGH), and GL ~66.3 — one of the highest glycaemic loads of any single-serving food in the Korean cuisine category. The GI ~85 reflects the near-complete gelatinisation of rice starch during tteok manufacturing, making the starch immediately accessible to amylase and producing a rapid, large postprandial glucose spike. Sodium per serving is approximately 1,200mg, contributed by gochujang sauce, processed fishcake, and soy sauce. Clinically, tteokbokki is classified as high-concern for both Type 2 Diabetes (extreme GL) and Hypertension (very high sodium). In Singapore, tteokbokki is widely available at Korean restaurants, Korean convenience stores (GS25, CU), and food delivery platforms; Ottogi and Haechandle instant tteokbokki kits are sold at Korean grocery stores for home preparation.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy rice cake and fishcake entries as a composite estimate; no single approved-source entry exists for tteokbokki as a complete dish. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database / International Tables of GI Values for rice cake and glutinous rice products. The composite adaptation is well-supported and internally consistent, justifying medium confidence.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for tteokbokki composite dish not available in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy rice cake or fishcake entries as of 2024. water_g set to -1: moisture content for the composite tteokbokki dish (rice cakes + sauce + fishcake) not reported as a single value in USDA FoodData Central. trans_fat_g is 0.0 (confirmed negligible per USDA SR Legacy rice cake and fishcake entries — no hydrogenated fats in traditional tteokbokki preparation). All core macros (protein_g 4.0g, fat_g 2.0g, carbohydrate_g 26.0g) are non-zero and sourced from USDA FoodData Central adapted composite. GI ~85 is an analogous estimate from International Tables of GI Values (Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care) for rice cake and glutinous rice products; no direct tteokbokki GI measurement exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GL per serving = (85 × 78g available carbohydrate per 300g serving) / 100 = 66.3. Sodium per serving ~1,200mg reflects gochujang sauce (~600mg), processed fishcake (~400mg), and soy sauce (~200mg) contributions. Halal status: traditional tteokbokki with fishcake is generally considered halal; however, gochujang brands vary — consumers should verify brand-specific halal certification. The input record listed 'diabetes: suitable = true' which appears to be a data error given GI ~85 and GL ~66.3; this has been corrected to 'high concern' in health_context. Rheumatoid arthritis condition from input record not carried forward as it is not a mandatory Project Veritas v2 health condition.
